mission.title: envoy: UDP proxying support
mission.description: UDP proxying support. This issue affects 39+ users.

steps.title: Identify envoy feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your envoy deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=envoy
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: UDP proxying support

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Edit: (@alyssawilk on behalf of @cmluciano)

## Design doc: 
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1G9IVq7F7Onwinsl6EYzGsdzAGvVbo2FGfcPt35ItIx8)

## Roadmap

steps.title: Apply the fix for UDP proxying support
steps.description:
This PR allows the statsd UDP server address to be set as a JSON config parameter. 

To allow for unit testing, the udp statsd writer_ has been changed from a static thread_local object to being derived from the ThreadLocal object class.

In this PR, `statsd_local_udp_port` has been DEPRECATED and r
```yaml
"bind_config": {
  "type": "udp",
  "address": "0.0.0.0:80"
}
```

steps.title: Confirm UDP proxying support is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: This PR allows the statsd UDP server address to be set as a JSON config parameter. 

To allow for unit testing, the udp statsd writer_ has been changed from a static thread_local object to being derived from the ThreadLocal object class.

In this PR, `statsd_local_udp_port` has been DEPRECATED and replaced with `statsd_udp_ip_address`.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
